risks: High-risk customers (Group 3) may require immediate attention to prevent churn or losses
Medium-risk customers (Group 2) could deteriorate into high-risk if not properly managed
narrative.summary: Customers naturally divide into three equal groups with distinct risk profiles. The segmentation shows clear separation with good quality, allowing for targeted business strategies based on customer risk levels.
opportunities: Low-risk customers (Group 1) represent a stable revenue base that can be leveraged for growth
Targeted interventions can be designed for each risk segment to optimize resource allocation

characteristics.description: Low engagement, at risk of leaving.

businessProfile.description: Retail business with customer purchase data, tracking how recently and frequently customers buy and how much they spend. Uses RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) metrics to understand customer value and behavior patterns.

featureRecommendations.why: Enables segmentation by product preferences and cross-selling opportunities
